page: Microsoft Azure NV24ads_V710_v5 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NVadsV710v5 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features an x86_64 AMD processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 5.33 GB of RAM per core.

The server includes a single V710 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM, designed to accelerate graphics and compute workloads. For local data storage, the instance is bundled with 1649 GB of local disk space and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of dedicated AMD vCPUs, a mid-range V710 GPU, and substantial local storage offers a balanced hardware profile for workloads requiring both compute and graphical acceleration. This server is suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, 3D rendering, CAD applications, and entry-level machine learning workloads that benefit from dedicated GPU resources and local high-capacity storage.
description: Microsoft Azure NV24ads_V710_v5 is an x86_64 GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NVadsV710v5 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. The server features 24 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of system memory (5.33 GB per core), and a single V710 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Storage needs are met by 1649 GB of local disk, with additional support for premium storage. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance provides a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and graphics acceleration. It is designed for workloads such as virtual desktops, 3D visualization, and GPU-assisted data processing.
